incidents.victim: US, Ukrainian, and other oil & gas operators
incidents.vector: Intrusions into protected industrial computers; conspiracy to damage protected systems plus wire fraud and identity theft.
incidents.impact: Russian national Artem Vladimirovich Revenskii (online handle 'Digit') pleaded guilty in US federal court to breaking into and damaging oil and gas facility systems in the US, Ukraine and other countries, facing up to 27 years in prison.

incidents.vector: Unauthorized intrusion into the national healthcare insurance database; no ransom demand reported.
incidents.impact: Personal and financial information on virtually every adult Moldovan citizen and on Moldovan private medical providers was stolen; CNAM said roughly 30% of the data was impacted (some had to be restored from backups). Cybersecurity Agency officials did not exclude Russian involvement.

incidents.vector: Phishing emails with malicious archives, fake aviation-software ad campaigns, and trojanized installers (including via SourceForge) used to deploy spyware that captures keystrokes, screenshots, browser data and Telegram credentials.
incidents.impact: Long-running campaign aimed at stealing geospatial / GIS data on Russian roads, infrastructure and strategic facilities; researchers also see lures targeting drone operators and Starlink users, suggesting military targeting.

incidents.victim: Brazilian internet service providers (small regional ISPs)
incidents.vector: Mirai-variant botnet built from TP-Link Archer AX21 routers vulnerable to CVE-2023-1389, abusing infrastructure and SSH keys belonging to a Brazilian DDoS-protection firm (Huge Networks) that was itself compromised in January 2026.
incidents.impact: Years of large DDoS attacks against Brazilian ISPs, with each victim prefix hit for 10-60 seconds in parallel. KrebsOnSecurity exposed an open archive showing root access to Huge Networks' systems and DNS-amplification scripts targeting only Brazilian IP ranges.

incidents.victim: Delta Dental Insurance Company / Delta Dental of New York
incidents.vector: Threat actors exploited the mid-2023 MOVEit Transfer zero-day to access file transfer servers used by the insurers.
incidents.impact: NYDFS announced a $2.25 million settlement for cybersecurity violations after the 2023 MOVEit breach exposed names, addresses, SSNs, driver's license numbers, financial account data and patient health records of New York consumers; the companies also failed to timely report the events.

incidents.victim: SAP enterprise developers / Cloud Application Programming ecosystem
incidents.vector: Compromised npm packages (@cap-js/sqlite, @cap-js/postgres, @cap-js/db-service, mbt) used a preinstall hook to download the Bun runtime and run an obfuscated 11.7 MB credential stealer.
incidents.impact: Worm-like payload harvested GitHub and npm tokens, AWS/Azure/GCP secrets, Kubernetes service-account tokens, Claude/MCP configs and VPN configs from CI/CD pipelines, then used GitHub commit search as a covert C2 to self-propagate. Affected packages have ~570K weekly downloads, putting many enterprise SAP developers and CI/CD systems at risk.

incidents.attacker: Criminal gang — voice-phishing extortion (BlackFile / Cordial Spider)
incidents.vector: Vishing impersonating IT support directs employees to adversary-in-the-middle SSO phishing pages; attackers steal session tokens, register their own MFA devices, then pivot through SaaS apps (Microsoft Graph, Salesforce APIs, SharePoint) and exfiltrate data.
incidents.impact: Active campaign since February against retailers and hotels (also healthcare, tech, transport) with seven-figure ransom demands and swatting of executives to increase pressure; victims are listed on the group's data-leak site.

incidents.victim: US federal civilian agency (Cisco Firepower / ASA users globally)
incidents.vector: Exploitation of Cisco ASA n-day vulnerabilities CVE-2025-20333 and CVE-2025-20362 to deploy the FIRESTARTER backdoor, which manipulates the Cisco Service Platform mount list to survive firmware patches and standard reboots.
incidents.impact: CISA and UK NCSC disclosed FIRESTARTER on a federal Firepower device that remained compromised after September 2025 patches were applied; attackers re-deployed Line Viper six months after initial breach. CISA updated Emergency Directive 25-03 forcing federal agencies to audit Cisco firewalls and submit memory snapshots; affects Firepower 1000/2100/4100/9300 and Secure Firewall 1200/3100/4200 series.

incidents.victim: Checkmarx software distribution channels and downstream developers
incidents.attacker: Criminal gang — supply-chain compromise (TeamPCP, 'Shai-Hulud: The Third Coming')
incidents.vector: Malicious @bitwarden/cli npm package (v2026.4.0) and poisoned Checkmarx Docker Hub images, GitHub Actions and VS Code extensions, all sharing the same C2 (audit.checkmarx[.]cx), obfuscation and credential-harvesting code.
incidents.impact: Multi-channel supply-chain campaign that steals cloud and developer credentials, then backdoors every npm package the victim can publish, automating self-propagation. CISA added entries to its KEV catalog and the campaign is treated as a continuation of Shai-Hulud and direct precursor to the SAP wave.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of an on-premises Microsoft Exchange server, part of a wider campaign that hit more than 5,000 Exchange servers this year.
incidents.impact: Chinese hackers breached the Cuban embassy's Exchange email server and stole diplomats' inboxes, reportedly to monitor US naval activity related to the Strait of Hormuz blockade.

incidents.vector: Lumma Stealer infection on a Context.ai employee (via a malicious 'Roblox cheat' download in February) gave attackers AWS access and OAuth tokens for a Vercel employee's Google Workspace account, enabling lateral movement into Vercel.
incidents.impact: Attackers reached Vercel environments and environment variables, exposing access keys, source code and database credentials for a small but unspecified number of Vercel customers; stolen data is being auctioned online. Vercel and Context.ai engaged CrowdStrike and Mandiant; the broader Context.ai OAuth app compromise potentially affects hundreds of organizations.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of a vulnerability in Kelp DAO's restaking infrastructure that allowed attackers to withdraw rsETH tokens.
incidents.impact: Approximately $292 million in crypto assets stolen — the year's largest crypto heist to date and TRM Labs' second nine-figure DPRK heist in less than three weeks. Combined with the Drift theft, North Korean actors have netted ~$575M in 18 days and now hold 76% of all crypto stolen in 2026.

incidents.vector: Cyberattack of unconfirmed type; surrounding Leon County preemptively disconnected its IT network from the city — a posture typically associated with ransomware containment.
incidents.impact: City IT network shut down, multiple city websites and public services offline; Leon County isolated its own systems to prevent contamination.

incidents.vector: Part of the same broader 2026 'ShinyHunters' campaign that compromised Vercel/Context.ai and other companies via OAuth-token and infostealer-driven access.
incidents.impact: Personal data of 7.5 million Carnival customers leaked online; the same campaign also affected Zara, 7-Eleven, Udemy and Medtronic according to reporting.

incidents.vector: Compromise of OneIndia's digital streaming infrastructure to broadcast a political message.
incidents.impact: Hacktivists broadcast a political message attacking India's ties to the Taliban regime to OneIndia's 30M+ subscribers across its streaming platforms.

incidents.vector: Ransomware intrusion followed by data exfiltration and leak.
incidents.impact: SafePay ransomware group hit Favelle Favco and leaked stolen company data on its leak site.

incidents.vector: Months-long social-engineering campaign by a DPRK APT (tracked publicly as Citrine Sleet / AppleJeus / UNC4736) culminating in compromise of Drift's infrastructure.
incidents.impact: Approximately $285-300 million in crypto stolen; combined with the April 18 Kelp DAO theft, the two heists account for the bulk of the $575M North Korea has now stolen in 2026.
